title: Get AdCP Capabilities Response
description: Response payload for get_adcp_capabilities task. Protocol-level capability discovery across all AdCP protocols. Each domain protocol has its own capability section.

major_versions.description: AdCP major versions supported by this seller. Major versions indicate breaking changes. When multiple versions are listed, the buyer declares its version via the adcp_major_version field on requests.

supported_protocols.description: Which AdCP domain protocols this seller supports

account.description: Account management capabilities. Describes how accounts are established, what billing models are supported, and whether an account is required before browsing products.

require_operator_auth.description: Whether the seller requires operator-level credentials. When true (explicit accounts), operators authenticate independently with the seller and the buyer discovers accounts via list_accounts. When false (default, implicit accounts), the seller trusts the agent's identity claims — the agent authenticates once and declares brands/operators via sync_accounts.

authorization_endpoint.description: OAuth authorization endpoint for obtaining operator-level credentials. Present when the seller supports OAuth for operator authentication. The agent directs the operator to this URL to authenticate and obtain a bearer token. If absent and require_operator_auth is true, operators obtain credentials out-of-band (e.g., seller portal, API key).

supported_billing.description: Billing models this seller supports. operator: seller invoices the operator (agency or brand buying direct). agent: agent consolidates billing. advertiser: seller invoices the advertiser directly, even when a different operator places orders on their behalf. The buyer must pass one of these values in sync_accounts.

required_for_products.description: Whether an account reference is required for get_products. When true, the buyer must establish an account before browsing products. When false (default), the buyer can browse products without an account — useful for price comparison and discovery before committing to a seller.

account_financials.description: Whether this seller supports the get_account_financials task for querying account-level financial status (spend, credit, invoices). Only applicable to operator-billed accounts.

sandbox.description: Whether this seller supports sandbox accounts for testing. Buyers can provision a sandbox account via sync_accounts with sandbox: true, and all requests using that account_id will be treated as sandbox — no real platform calls or spend.

media_buy.description: Media-buy protocol capabilities. Only present if media_buy is in supported_protocols.

supported_pricing_models.description: Pricing models this seller supports across its product portfolio. Buyers can use this for pre-flight filtering before querying individual products. Individual products may support a subset of these models.

items.title: Pricing Model
items.description: Supported pricing models for advertising products

enumDescriptions.cpm: Cost Per Mille - cost per 1,000 impressions
enumDescriptions.vcpm: Viewable Cost Per Mille - cost per 1,000 viewable impressions (MRC standard)
enumDescriptions.cpc: Cost Per Click - cost per click on the ad
enumDescriptions.cpcv: Cost Per Completed View - cost per 100% video/audio completion
enumDescriptions.cpv: Cost Per View - cost per view at publisher-defined threshold (e.g., 50% completion)
enumDescriptions.cpp: Cost Per Point - cost per Gross Rating Point or Target Rating Point (TV/audio)
enumDescriptions.cpa: Cost Per Acquisition - cost per conversion event (purchase, lead, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.flat_rate: Flat Rate - fixed cost regardless of delivery volume (sponsorships, takeovers)
enumDescriptions.time: Time - cost per time unit (hour, day, week, or month) that scales with campaign duration

reporting.description: Seller-level reporting capabilities. Summarizes what reporting features are available across the seller's product portfolio. Individual products may vary — check product-level reporting_capabilities for specifics.

supports_date_range.description: Whether any products support date range filtering (date_range_support: 'date_range'). When false, all products return lifetime-only data.

supports_daily_breakdown.description: Whether delivery reporting includes daily_breakdown at the media buy and/or package level.

supports_webhooks.description: Whether any products support webhook-based reporting notifications.

available_dimensions.description: Reporting dimensions available across the seller's portfolio. Individual products may support a subset. Dimensions geo, device_type, device_platform, audience, and placement are opt-in via reporting_dimensions on the delivery request. Dimensions creative, keyword, and catalog_item are included automatically when the seller supports them (not controlled by reporting_dimensions).

features.title: Media Buy Features
features.description: Optional media-buy protocol features. Used in capability declarations (seller declares support) and product filters (buyer requires support). If a seller declares a feature as true, they MUST honor requests using that feature.

inline_creative_management.description: Supports creatives provided inline in create_media_buy requests

property_list_filtering.description: Honors property_list parameter in get_products to filter results to buyer-approved properties

content_standards.description: Full support for content_standards configuration including sampling rates and category filtering

conversion_tracking.description: Supports sync_event_sources and log_event tasks for conversion event tracking

audience_targeting.description: Supports sync_audiences task and audience_include/audience_exclude in targeting overlays for first-party CRM audience management

catalog_management.description: Supports sync_catalogs task for catalog feed management with platform review and approval

execution.description: Technical execution capabilities for media buying

trusted_match.description: Trusted Match Protocol (TMP) support. When present, this seller supports real-time contextual and/or identity matching. Check individual products via get_products for per-product TMP capabilities.

supported.description: Whether this seller has TMP infrastructure deployed.

surfaces.description: Surface types this seller supports via TMP.

axe_integrations.description: Deprecated. Legacy AXE integrations. Use trusted_match for new integrations.

vast_versions.description: VAST versions supported for video creatives

mraid_versions.description: MRAID versions supported for rich media mobile creatives

vpaid.description: VPAID support for interactive video ads

simid.description: SIMID support for interactive video ads

targeting.description: Targeting capabilities. If declared true/supported, buyer can use these targeting parameters and seller MUST honor them.

geo_countries.description: Supports country-level geo targeting using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (e.g., 'US', 'GB', 'DE')

geo_regions.description: Supports region/state-level geo targeting using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., 'US-NY', 'GB-SCT', 'DE-BY')

geo_metros.description: Metro area targeting support. Specifies which classification systems are supported.

nielsen_dma.description: Supports Nielsen DMA codes (US market, e.g., '501' for NYC)

geo_postal_areas.description: Postal area targeting support. Specifies which postal code systems are supported. System names encode country and precision.

age_restriction.description: Age restriction capabilities for compliance (alcohol, gambling)

verification_methods.description: Age verification methods this seller supports

items.title: Age Verification Method
items.description: Methods for verifying user age for compliance. Does not include 'inferred' as it is not accepted for regulatory compliance.

device_platform.description: Whether seller supports device platform targeting (Sec-CH-UA-Platform values)

device_type.description: Whether seller supports device type targeting (form factor: desktop, mobile, tablet, ctv, dooh, unknown). When true, seller supports both device_type (include) and device_type_exclude (exclude) in targeting overlays.

language.description: Whether seller supports language targeting (ISO 639-1 codes)

audience_include.description: Whether seller supports audience_include in targeting overlays (requires features.audience_targeting)

audience_exclude.description: Whether seller supports audience_exclude in targeting overlays (requires features.audience_targeting)

keyword_targets.description: Keyword targeting capabilities. Presence indicates support for targeting_overlay.keyword_targets and keyword_targets_add/remove in update_media_buy.

supported_match_types.description: Match types this seller supports for keyword targets. Sellers must reject goals with unsupported match types.

negative_keywords.description: Negative keyword capabilities. Presence indicates support for targeting_overlay.negative_keywords and negative_keywords_add/remove in update_media_buy.

supported_match_types.description: Match types this seller supports for negative keywords. Sellers must reject goals with unsupported match types.

geo_proximity.description: Proximity targeting capabilities from arbitrary coordinates via targeting_overlay.geo_proximity.

radius.description: Whether seller supports simple radius targeting (distance circle from a point)

travel_time.description: Whether seller supports travel time isochrone targeting (requires a routing engine)

geometry.description: Whether seller supports pre-computed GeoJSON geometry (buyer provides the polygon)

transport_modes.description: Transport modes supported for travel_time isochrones. Only relevant when travel_time is true.

items.title: Transport Mode
items.description: Transportation modes for isochrone-based catchment area calculations. Determines how travel time translates to geographic reach.

enumDescriptions.walking: Pedestrian travel on walkable paths and sidewalks
enumDescriptions.cycling: Bicycle travel on roads and cycle paths
enumDescriptions.driving: Motor vehicle travel on road networks
enumDescriptions.public_transport: Public transit including bus, rail, metro, and ferry

audience_targeting.description: Audience targeting capabilities. Only present when features.audience_targeting is true.

supported_identifier_types.description: PII-derived identifier types accepted for audience matching. Buyers should only send identifiers the seller supports.

supports_platform_customer_id.description: Whether the seller accepts the buyer's CRM/loyalty ID as a matchable identifier. Only applicable when the seller operates a closed ecosystem with a shared ID namespace (e.g., a retailer matching against their loyalty program). When true, buyers can include platform_customer_id values in AudienceMember.identifiers for matching against the seller's identity graph. Reporting on matched platform_customer_ids typically requires a clean room or the seller's own reporting surface.

supported_uid_types.description: Universal ID types accepted for audience matching (MAIDs, RampID, UID2, etc.). MAID support varies significantly by platform — check this field before sending uids with type: maid.

items.title: UID Type
items.description: Type of user identifier. Used in audience sync, event logging, and TMP identity match requests to tell the receiver which identity graph to resolve against.

enumDescriptions.pairid: Publisher Addressable Identity via IAB Tech Lab PAIR protocol
enumDescriptions.publisher_first_party: Publisher's own first-party identifier, opaque to buyers
enumDescriptions.other: Other universal ID type (specify in ext)

minimum_audience_size.description: Minimum matched audience size required for targeting. Audiences below this threshold will have status: too_small. Varies by platform (100–1000 is typical).

matching_latency_hours.description: Expected matching latency range in hours after upload. Use to calibrate polling cadence and set appropriate expectations before configuring push_notification_config.

conversion_tracking.description: Seller-level conversion tracking capabilities. Only present when features.conversion_tracking is true.

multi_source_event_dedup.description: Whether this seller can deduplicate conversion events across multiple event sources within a single goal. When true, the seller honors the deduplication semantics in optimization_goals event_sources arrays — the same event_id from multiple sources counts once. When false or absent, buyers should use a single event source per goal; multi-source arrays will be treated as first-source-wins. Most social platforms cannot deduplicate across independently-managed pixel and CAPI sources.

supported_event_types.description: Event types this seller can track and attribute. If omitted, all standard event types are supported.

items.title: Event Type
items.description: Standard marketing event types for event logging, aligned with IAB ECAPI

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

supported_uid_types.description: Universal ID types accepted for user matching

items.title: UID Type
items.description: Type of user identifier. Used in audience sync, event logging, and TMP identity match requests to tell the receiver which identity graph to resolve against.

enumDescriptions.pairid: Publisher Addressable Identity via IAB Tech Lab PAIR protocol
enumDescriptions.publisher_first_party: Publisher's own first-party identifier, opaque to buyers
enumDescriptions.other: Other universal ID type (specify in ext)

supported_hashed_identifiers.description: Hashed PII types accepted for user matching. Buyers must hash before sending (SHA-256, normalized).

supported_action_sources.description: Action sources this seller accepts events from

enumDescriptions.app: Event occurred in a mobile or desktop app
enumDescriptions.phone_call: Event originated from a phone call
enumDescriptions.chat: Event originated from a chat conversation
enumDescriptions.email: Event originated from an email interaction
enumDescriptions.in_store: Event occurred at a physical retail location
enumDescriptions.system_generated: Event generated by an automated system

attribution_windows.description: Attribution windows available from this seller. Single-element arrays indicate fixed windows; multi-element arrays indicate configurable options the buyer can choose from via attribution_window on optimization goals.

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Event type this window applies to, or omit for default window

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

items.title: Duration
items.description: A time duration expressed as an interval and unit. Used for frequency cap windows, attribution windows, reach optimization windows, time budgets, and other time-based settings. When unit is 'campaign', interval must be 1 — the window spans the full campaign flight.

interval.description: Number of time units. Must be 1 when unit is 'campaign'.

unit.description: Time unit. 'seconds' for sub-minute precision. 'campaign' spans the full campaign flight.

items.title: Duration
items.description: A time duration expressed as an interval and unit. Used for frequency cap windows, attribution windows, reach optimization windows, time budgets, and other time-based settings. When unit is 'campaign', interval must be 1 — the window spans the full campaign flight.

interval.description: Number of time units. Must be 1 when unit is 'campaign'.

unit.description: Time unit. 'seconds' for sub-minute precision. 'campaign' spans the full campaign flight.

content_standards_detail.description: Content standards implementation details. Only meaningful when features.content_standards is true. Gives buyers pre-buy visibility into local evaluation and artifact delivery capabilities.

supports_local_evaluation.description: Whether the seller runs a local evaluation model. When false, all artifacts will have local_verdict: 'unevaluated' and the failures_only filter on get_media_buy_artifacts is not useful.

supported_channels.description: Channels for which the seller can provide content artifacts. Helps buyers understand which parts of a mixed-channel buy will have content standards coverage.

items.title: Media Channel
items.description: Standardized advertising media channels describing how buyers allocate budget. Channels are planning abstractions, not technical substrates. See the Media Channel Taxonomy specification for detailed definitions.

enumDescriptions.display: Digital display advertising (banners, native, rich media) across web and app
enumDescriptions.olv: Online video advertising outside CTV (pre-roll, outstream, in-app video)
enumDescriptions.social: Social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, etc.)
enumDescriptions.search: Search engine advertising and search networks
enumDescriptions.ctv: Connected TV and streaming on television screens
enumDescriptions.streaming_audio: Digital audio streaming services (Spotify, Pandora, etc.)
enumDescriptions.dooh: Digital out-of-home screens in public spaces
enumDescriptions.print: Newspapers, magazines, and other print publications
enumDescriptions.email: Email advertising and sponsored newsletter content
enumDescriptions.retail_media: Retail media networks and commerce marketplaces (Amazon, Walmart, Instacart)
enumDescriptions.affiliate: Affiliate networks, comparison sites, and performance-based partnerships
enumDescriptions.product_placement: Product placement, branded content, and sponsorship integrations
enumDescriptions.sponsored_intelligence: Sponsored Intelligence — advertising within AI assistants, AI search, and generative AI experiences via the reversed data flow

supports_webhook_delivery.description: Whether the seller supports push-based artifact delivery via artifact_webhook configured at buy creation time.

portfolio.description: Information about the seller's media inventory portfolio

publisher_domains.description: Publisher domains this seller is authorized to represent. Buyers should fetch each publisher's adagents.json for property definitions.

primary_channels.description: Primary advertising channels in this portfolio

items.title: Media Channel
items.description: Standardized advertising media channels describing how buyers allocate budget. Channels are planning abstractions, not technical substrates. See the Media Channel Taxonomy specification for detailed definitions.

enumDescriptions.display: Digital display advertising (banners, native, rich media) across web and app
enumDescriptions.olv: Online video advertising outside CTV (pre-roll, outstream, in-app video)
enumDescriptions.social: Social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, etc.)
enumDescriptions.search: Search engine advertising and search networks
enumDescriptions.ctv: Connected TV and streaming on television screens
enumDescriptions.streaming_audio: Digital audio streaming services (Spotify, Pandora, etc.)
enumDescriptions.dooh: Digital out-of-home screens in public spaces
enumDescriptions.print: Newspapers, magazines, and other print publications
enumDescriptions.email: Email advertising and sponsored newsletter content
enumDescriptions.retail_media: Retail media networks and commerce marketplaces (Amazon, Walmart, Instacart)
enumDescriptions.affiliate: Affiliate networks, comparison sites, and performance-based partnerships
enumDescriptions.product_placement: Product placement, branded content, and sponsorship integrations
enumDescriptions.sponsored_intelligence: Sponsored Intelligence — advertising within AI assistants, AI search, and generative AI experiences via the reversed data flow

primary_countries.description: Primary countries (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) where inventory is concentrated

description.description: Markdown-formatted description of the inventory portfolio

advertising_policies.description: Advertising content policies, restrictions, and guidelines

signals.description: Signals protocol capabilities. Only present if signals is in supported_protocols.

data_provider_domains.description: Data provider domains this signals agent is authorized to resell. Buyers should fetch each data provider's adagents.json for signal catalog definitions and to verify authorization.

catalog_signals.description: Supports signals from data provider catalogs with structured signal_id references

governance.description: Governance protocol capabilities. Only present if governance is in supported_protocols. Governance agents provide property and creative data like compliance scores, brand safety ratings, sustainability metrics, and creative quality assessments.

property_features.description: Property features this governance agent can evaluate. Each feature describes a score, rating, or certification the agent can provide for properties.

type.description: Data type: 'binary' for yes/no, 'quantitative' for numeric scores, 'categorical' for enum values

range.description: For quantitative features, the valid range

categories.description: For categorical features, the valid values

description.description: Human-readable description of what this feature measures

methodology_url.description: URL to documentation explaining how this feature is calculated or measured. Helps buyers understand and compare methodologies across vendors.

creative_features.description: Creative features this governance agent can evaluate. Each feature describes a score, rating, or assessment the agent can provide for creatives (e.g., security scanning, creative quality, content categorization).

type.description: Data type: 'binary' for yes/no, 'quantitative' for numeric scores, 'categorical' for enum values

range.description: For quantitative features, the valid range

categories.description: For categorical features, the valid values

description.description: Human-readable description of what this feature measures

methodology_url.description: URL to documentation explaining how this feature is calculated or measured.

sponsored_intelligence.description: Sponsored Intelligence protocol capabilities. Only present if sponsored_intelligence is in supported_protocols. SI agents handle conversational brand experiences.

transports.description: Available protocol transports. Hosts select based on their capabilities.

url.description: Agent endpoint URL for this transport

preferred.description: Preferred transport when host supports multiple

conversational.description: Pure text exchange - the baseline modality

avatar.description: Animated video presence with brand avatar

standard.description: Standard components that all SI hosts must render

acp_checkout.description: Supports ACP (Agentic Commerce Protocol) checkout handoff

mcp_apps.description: Supports MCP Apps for rendering A2UI surfaces in iframes

brand_url.description: URL to brand.json with colors, fonts, logos, tone

brand.description: Brand protocol capabilities. Only present if brand is in supported_protocols. Brand agents provide identity data (logos, colors, tone, assets) and optionally rights clearance for licensable content (talent, music, stock media).

identity.description: Supports get_brand_identity for retrieving brand identity data

rights.description: Supports get_rights and acquire_rights for rights discovery and clearance

right_types.description: Types of rights available through this agent

items.title: Right Type
items.description: Categories of intellectual property rights that can be licensed through the brand protocol.

enumDescriptions.talent: Rights to a real person's likeness, voice, name, or endorsement (athletes, celebrities, influencers)
enumDescriptions.character: Rights to fictional characters for advertising or co-branding
enumDescriptions.brand_ip: Rights to brand intellectual property (logos, trademarks, trade dress)
enumDescriptions.music: Rights to musical compositions or recordings (sync, background music)
enumDescriptions.stock_media: Rights to stock photography, video, or audio collections

available_uses.description: Rights uses available across this agent's roster

items.title: Right Use
items.description: Types of rights usage that can be licensed through the brand protocol. Aligned with DDEX UseType direction for interoperability with music and media rights systems.

enumDescriptions.likeness: Visual representation of a person's appearance, including AI-generated imagery
enumDescriptions.voice: Audio reproduction of a person's voice, including AI-generated speech
enumDescriptions.name: Use of a person's name in advertising or promotional materials
enumDescriptions.endorsement: Implied or explicit endorsement of a product or service by a person
enumDescriptions.motion_capture: Use of a person's movement patterns for animation or video
enumDescriptions.signature: Use of a person's signature or autograph
enumDescriptions.catchphrase: Use of a person's well-known phrases or sayings
enumDescriptions.sync: Synchronization of music with visual media (ads, video)
enumDescriptions.background_music: Use of music as background audio in advertising
enumDescriptions.editorial: Use in editorial or informational contexts
enumDescriptions.commercial: Use in commercial advertising and promotional contexts

generation_providers.description: LLM/generation providers this agent can issue credentials for

description.description: Description of the agent's brand protocol capabilities

creative.description: Creative protocol capabilities. Only present if creative is in supported_protocols.

supports_compliance.description: When true, this creative agent can process briefs with compliance requirements (required_disclosures, prohibited_claims) and will validate that disclosures can be satisfied by the target format.

has_creative_library.description: When true, this agent hosts a creative library and supports list_creatives and creative_id references in build_creative. Creative agents with a library should also implement the accounts protocol (sync_accounts / list_accounts) so buyers can establish access.

supports_generation.description: When true, this agent can generate creatives from natural language briefs via build_creative. The buyer provides a message with creative direction, and the agent produces a manifest with generated assets. When false, build_creative only supports transformation or library retrieval.

supports_transformation.description: When true, this agent can transform or resize existing manifests via build_creative. The buyer provides a creative_manifest and a target_format_id, and the agent adapts the creative to the new format.

extensions_supported.description: Extension namespaces this agent supports. Buyers can expect meaningful data in ext.{namespace} fields on responses from this agent. Extension schemas are published in the AdCP extension registry.

items.description: Extension namespace (lowercase alphanumeric with underscores, e.g., 'scope3', 'garm', 'iab_tcf')

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when capabilities were last updated. Buyers can use this for cache invalidation.

items.title: Error
items.description: Standard error structure for task-specific errors and warnings

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Standard codes are defined in error-code.json and enable autonomous agent recovery. Sellers MAY use codes not in the standard vocabulary for platform-specific errors; agents MUST handle unknown codes gracefully by falling back to the recovery classification.

field.description: Field path associated with the error (e.g., 'packages[0].targeting')

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying the operation. Sellers MUST return values between 1 and 3600. Clients MUST clamp values outside this range.

recovery.description: Agent recovery classification. transient: retry after delay (rate limit, service unavailable, timeout). correctable: fix the request and resend (invalid field, budget too low, creative rejected). terminal: requires human action (account suspended, payment required, account not found).

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
